own_review.point: System.exit(-1) added inside shared core-module utility ScanUtil; it is called not only from FluoScan.main but also from the deprecated-but-shipped AppRunner.scan (modules/cluster), an instance method on a library class. Killing the JVM from a util prevents callers from handling failure; should throw and let the CLI entry point decide exit codes.
In scanFluo the new System.exit(-1) sits inside the try-with-resources for FluoClient; System.exit does not unwind the stack, so client.close() never runs (unclean ZK/Accumulo shutdown).

The 'Scanning...' headers and 'No data found' feedback are deleted outright; per the issue intent (clean pipeable stdout) they could be printed to System.err instead — silent empty output is a UX regression.
Stray blank lines left at the start of both method bodies and where entriesFound++ was removed (style).
No tests added/updated for the behavior change; existing ScanTest only covers getSpan/getColumns.

(matches human) The stderr/System.exit change is unrelated to the stated goal of issue #981 (removing meta output) — it is a separate exit-code bug fix bundled into the PR and worth calling out as out of scope.

points.point: PR title misleading, changes REMOVE meta info rather than restore it
points.why: Misreading: title 'stopped printing meta info' describes what the PR does (issue's stated goal was removal, confirmed by human review); no contradiction exists.

points.point: Removing 'No data found' leaves users with no feedback on empty scan
points.why: Real UX regression; matches my own finding.

points.why: Praise/restating the diff (claims verified, but still praise).

points.why: Duplicate praise.

points.why: Flags the real library-code exit risk, though it defuses it as 'acceptable'.

points.why: Restates the diff / praise.

points.point: CommandUtil.java:28-30,38-40 still prints errors to stdout — follow-up for consistency

System.exit in shared util harms non-CLI callers (actively argued it's fine)
Removed 'Scanning...'/'No data found' output UX regression — not mentioned at all
exceeds: CommandUtil.java stdout-error inconsistency (verified; neither human nor my review found it)

qualitative: v3 is the best here (net 0): it is the only candidate to clearly state the real risk — System.exit(-1) added to a shared core-module utility that the deprecated AppRunner embeds — with the correct remedy (throw instead), and it also caught the test-coverage gap and the No-data-found UX regression. v1 hedged the same exit concern into 'acceptable, add a comment' and padded with praise; v2 never mentioned the exit risk at all and spent its points on UX and convention praise. v4 actively argued the exit was 'appropriate' and ignored the message-removal UX entirely, but it earned the only genuine 'exceeds' finding: the verified CommandUtil stdout-error inconsistency on the very code path FluoScan uses. Characteristically, v1, v2 and v3 all fabricated the same 'PR title contradicts the diff' complaint — a misreading, since the issue's stated goal (confirmed by the human review) was precisely to stop printing meta info. All four missed the human's actual point (the exit-code change is an unrelated bundled bug fix) and the subtler defect that System.exit inside the try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close().
